description: 9:16 hard horizontal split: top half rotates between screen recording / bold-headline card / custom dashboard; bottom half is a continuous naturally-graded speaker. Bold-sans captions with single-word brand-accent emphasis. Hard cuts only — no whip-blurs. Workmanlike ad/tutorial register. Map source-reel creator colors to your own --primary-mid / --accent-warm.
fits_script_when: script demos a tool live (Cursor, Claude Code, Notion, etc.) — live screen recording is essential
script is ad-style with a bold headline + product surface (promise → use-cases → CTA)
speaker needs to be the protagonist throughout, not a recessive narrator
topic is AI tooling / SaaS demo / agentic system / vibe-coding tutorial
ends with a comment or URL CTA where speaker fills the full frame
total duration is 30-220 seconds (ad-register short or long-form tutorial)
register is workmanlike — fast, substance-driven, not designed/premium
does_not_fit_when: script is essay or framework explanation with no live tool demo (use editorial-magazine)
script enumerates a list of design-rich concepts that need 3D chrome cards (use sage-stack)
no live tool demo, product UI, or headline-card surface to fill the top half
format calls for whip-blurs, light-leaks, or chromatic aberration (use sage-stack)
speaker should be desaturated / recessive (use sage-stack)
speaker is walking-and-talking outdoors (no archetype yet covers this)

patterns.name: Hard split — top: screen recording, bottom: speaker
patterns.description: Top 50% is a real screen recording (Cursor, Claude Code, Airtable, GitHub, Notion, ChatGPT). Real cursor visible. Bottom 50% is the continuous naturally-graded speaker. Hard cut in/out — no transitions. The workhorse beat for tool demos.

trigger.script_signal: speaker references a specific tool's UI, says 'look at this', 'I use [tool] to', or describes an action visible on a real screen recording
trigger.regex_hint: (look at|here'?s|i use|let me show|watch this|notice|see how|in (cursor|claude code|notion|airtable|github|chatgpt|figma|obsidian))

screen_rec_path.description: Path to the screen-recording mp4 / image. Flag as asset-needed if not provided.

letterbox_color.description: Color of any leftover top-half space when the screen rec doesn't fill it (defaults to pure black per archetype)

rounded_corners.description: Add 18px border-radius + drop-shadow for floating-device feel (optional)

split_ratio.description: Top-half height as fraction of frame. 0.5 default; 0.52 / 0.55 also valid.

examples.note: Krause's Meta Ads stack — Airtable Grid View + GitHub repo as Mode A surfaces
Long-form agentic-AI tutorial — heavy Mode A across 216s
Lindy ad — inbox screenshot, calendar UI, Lindy.ai signup page

patterns.name: Hard split — top: bold-sans headline card, bottom: speaker
patterns.description: Top 50% is a bold-sans headline on flat color (cream / off-white). Two-tone typography: ink (--anchor-near-black) + accent (--primary-mid default, --accent-warm for premium). Small-caps kicker subtitle above. Lands, holds 3-5s, hard cut. Used for hooks, promises, social-proof slabs.

trigger.script_signal: speaker delivers a punchline, promise, headline number, or social-proof claim — a sentence that benefits from being read more than heard

kicker_text.description: Small-caps subtitle in accent color. Letter-spaced 0.18em. Usually 2-4 words.

ink_text.description: Words in ink color (--anchor-near-black). The structural part of the headline.

accent_text.description: Words in accent color (the punchline word/phrase — number, verb, payoff)

accent_token.description: Accent color token. Default --primary-mid; use --accent-warm for premium / urgency / money beats only.

background_color.description: Headline-card background. Default warm-cream; off-white #f0eeea or pure white also valid.

examples.note: 'Save 60 Hours & $2,400' on cream — gold accent under Lindy brand (under your brand becomes --accent-warm)
'Lindy automates the boring stuff' — ink + gold split
Social-proof '10,000+ teams' headline slab

patterns.name: Hard split — top: custom dashboard / diagram, bottom: speaker
patterns.description: Top 50% is a custom-rendered metric dashboard / flowchart / message-bubble UI on dark charcoal (#0a0e14). One accent-color line stroke or chart curve with a subtle drop-shadow glow in the brand accent. Used to demonstrate WHAT a system does without needing a real screen recording.

trigger.script_signal: speaker explains a system, flow, or metric — references concepts like 'agentic OS', 'pipeline', 'flowchart', 'this graph', 'cumulative', or describes architecture/automation visually

header_text.description: Top-line caps label (e.g. 'AGENTIC OS · CUMULATIVE ACTIVITY · 30D')

stat_pairs.description: Comma-separated stat pairs for the stats row (e.g. '113 TOTAL, 113 LAST 30D'). Optional.

accent_token.description: Stroke / glow color for the chart line or flowchart edges. Default --primary-mid; --accent-warm for premium beats.

integration_tags.description: Comma-separated mono-font tags for the footer (e.g. 'GITHUB, GMAIL, GOOGLE DRIVE'). Optional.

examples.note: Carpathia Agentic OS — orange line chart on dark charcoal with integration tags
Lindy lead-enrich — message bubble + LinkedIn panel on peach gradient
Lindy email-triage flowchart
Dark teal Agent flowchart + n8n-style automation graph

patterns.name: Floating B-roll card overlay (sub-pattern, limited references)
patterns.description: Sub-pattern variant: speaker fills the full frame; B-roll cards (screen recording / headline card / dashboard) float as an overlay over the upper portion of the frame instead of taking a hard 50% split. Same naturally-graded speaker, same caption system. Single source example so far — flagged as a sub-pattern that may split into its own archetype if more examples emerge.

trigger.script_signal: format demands the speaker stay full-frame for connection while still showing a tool / surface — the intimacy of full-screen face combined with the demonstration of Mode A/B/C content

overlay_asset.description: Path to the B-roll asset to float over the upper frame

overlay_y_pct.description: Top offset as % of frame height for the overlay card

overlay_height_pct.description: Height of overlay card as % of frame height (smaller than 50% so speaker remains the spine)

overlay_shadow.description: Soft drop shadow under the overlay to lift it off the speaker plate

examples.note: ONLY current source example — speaker is full-frame, B-roll cards float over the upper frame as overlays. Watch for second example before promoting this from sub-pattern to first-class.

patterns.name: Bold-sans caption with single-word brand-accent emphasis (THE signature)
patterns.description: The signature move of this archetype. Bold sans-serif captions (Inter Black, Geist Black, ≥800 weight) sitting at y=65-75% in the speaker half. White words + ONE word per group in brand accent. Tight letter-spacing (-0.015em), heavy text-shadow for legibility. Pill / stroke / plain backdrop variants. The accent_token is set ONCE per reel — never switch mid-reel.

trigger.script_signal: any spoken word group during a Mode A / B / C beat — captions tick forward at speaker's syllable pace, 1-3 words per group, with one emphasis word per group (usually the noun, verb, brand name, or number)

word_groups.description: JSON array of {text, start_s, end_s, emphasis_index} objects — one per caption group; emphasis_index points to which word in the group gets accent color

accent_token.description: Per-reel accent color. Default --primary-mid; --accent-warm for entire reel only when topic is premium / urgency / money. Avoid the source-reel creator-coded colors. Set ONCE per reel — never switch mid-reel.

font_family.description: Heavy sans family. Inter Black, Geist Black, SF Pro Display Heavy. Weight must be ≥800.

backdrop_variant.description: plain (text-shadow only) | pill (rgba(20,20,20,.82) rounded-pill bg) | stroke (6px black -webkit-text-stroke for busy backgrounds)

y_position_pct.description: Vertical caption position as % of frame height. 65-75% = mid-torso of speaker.

font_size_px.description: Caption font size at 1080px width. 64-96px range.

examples.note: Plain backdrop variant — cyan emphasis under Krause brand (becomes --primary-mid under your brand)
Stroke variant — 6px black outline around cyan-emphasis caps captions
Pill variant — 'drowning in emails' with **emails** in gold (becomes --accent-warm under your brand for premium beats)
Hot red emphasis under Carpathia brand (becomes --primary-mid or --accent-warm under your brand, never red)

patterns.name: Word-by-word caption reveal with emphasis pulse
patterns.description: Animation layer for bold-sans-emphasis-caption. Each word group ticks forward at 0.4-0.8s pace; per-word opacity snap-on (no ease, captions are diegetic). The emphasis word gets a 0.18s scale-pulse (1.18 → 1.0, power3.out) the instant it appears — the secret-sauce micro-animation that reads as *emphasis* rather than animation.

trigger.script_signal: fires alongside every bold-sans-emphasis-caption group — this is the motion behavior, not a standalone scene

word_pace_s.description: Average duration per word group in seconds. 0.4-0.8s range matches normal speech pacing.

emphasis_pulse_scale.description: Starting scale for the emphasis-word pulse before settling to 1.0

reveal_ease.description: Easing for word-opacity reveal. Default 'none' (snap-on). Captions are diegetic, not animated.

examples.note: Word groups ticking ~0.6s, emphasis word punching with scale pulse on appearance
Slightly faster pacing for 216s tutorial — closer to 0.4-0.5s per group

patterns.name: Hard cut between top-half scenes (anti-pattern marker)
patterns.description: The format prescribes the *absence* of fancy transitions. Top-half mode swaps via instant visibility flip — `gsap.set(prev, { autoAlpha: 0 })` and `gsap.set(next, { autoAlpha: 1 })` at the cut frame. Optional 80ms cross-fade for related top-half scenes (e.g. scrolling Airtable → zoomed Airtable detail). NEVER whip-blur, glitch, light-leak, or chromatic aberration — those break the workmanlike register and belong to other archetypes.

trigger.script_signal: scene cut between two top-half beats — used by default 95% of the time in this archetype

variant.description: hard-cut (instant, default ~95%) | cross-fade-80ms (linear cross-fade for related scenes)

examples.note: Lindy ad — every top-half swap is a hard cut. Speaker stays continuous.
Same — hard cuts on Mode A → Mode A scrolling-detail transitions

patterns.name: Full-screen speaker close (CTA / climax beat)
patterns.description: Final beat: top half drops away (slides up off-screen), speaker video scales the wrapper from 50% → 100% height. Tight head-and-shoulders crop fills the frame. Speaker delivers the engagement instruction (comment X, sign up at URL, trust me on this). Caption is short, no color emphasis — the face IS the emphasis. Hold 1.5-3s to the end of the reel.

trigger.script_signal: speaker delivers an engagement instruction — comment, follow, save, sign up, go to [URL]. Often phrased 'just comment', 'follow for more', 'sign up at', 'trust me on this'.
trigger.regex_hint: (comment|follow|save this|sign up|go to|drop a|leave a|trust me|just|click)

cta_text.description: Caption text shown over the full-screen speaker. Short — usually 2-4 words. No color emphasis.

speaker_crop.description: Framing for the full-screen view. head-and-shoulders is tightest; chest-up if speaker prefers more breathing room.

examples.note: 'just comment' — top half drops, speaker fills frame for the close
Carpathia 'look me in the eye' close — full-screen speaker for the engagement ask

patterns.name: Caption-only fallback (no top-half graphic)
patterns.description: Safety-net pattern. When no other pattern matches a beat, this fires: plain bold-sans white captions with single-word --primary-mid emphasis sit over the speaker, no top-half graphic. Used for connector beats, simple claims, transitions without dedicated patterns. The speaker fills the full frame OR the top half goes pure black.

trigger.script_signal: fallback when no other pattern scores above 0.5

fallback_mode.description: black-top-half (top half goes #000, captions over speaker bottom half) | full-frame-speaker (top half drops, speaker fills frame, captions at y=68%)

emphasis_words.description: Comma-separated list of words to highlight in --primary-mid (the rest stay white)

accent_token.description: Accent color for emphasis word. Inherits the reel-level --accent.

examples.note: Used as the cohesive caption layer running underneath all top-half cuts
Long stretches of plain captions over speaker between Mode A/B/C beats

open_questions: floating-overlay-variant has only ONE source example. If it appears in 2+ more reels, promote it from sub-pattern to its own archetype (`speaker-overlay`) — the geometry is meaningfully different (no hard split).

Should hard-split-mode-a/b/c be ONE pattern with a `mode` param instead of three? Argued for split because each mode has materially different params and triggers cleanly off different script signals. Revisit if patterns.json grows unwieldy.
word-level-reveal-animation could fold into bold-sans-emphasis-caption since they always co-fire. Kept separate for now to make the motion behavior reusable.

Caption backdrop variant (plain / pill / stroke) — observed three variants but no clear rule for when to pick which. Hypothesis: pill works on light backgrounds, stroke works on busy backgrounds, plain works on plain dark. Need more samples to confirm.
Mode-B headline-card duration felt 3-5s but might be as short as 2s on faster-register reels. The current 2.5-5.0s range may need narrowing to 3-5s after frame-accurate timeline data lands.
